conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The elevator in my building has been out of service since Monday.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I switched my podcast subscription to a cheaper plan.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I finally organized the pile of mail that's been sitting on my counter.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I've been meaning to reorganize the garage for weeks now.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My cousin's baby shower is coming up, need to pick a gift.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My upstairs neighbor had some furniture delivered today, lots of noise.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to drop off some old clothes at the donation center.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Picking a message queue for the new event pipeline -- Kafka, RabbitMQ, or just SQS.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The office is switching to a new time-tracking tool next month.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My laptop update took forever to install last night.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Kafka has the throughput and retention story we'd eventually want, but it's a lot to operate for our current scale.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My phone's storage is almost full again, need to clear out old photos.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I've been meaning to water the plants on my desk more consistently.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: RabbitMQ is lighter weight than Kafka but we'd still be running and patching another service ourselves.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I finally returned that overdue library book.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to return a package that didn't fit.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to renew my gym membership before it lapses.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: SQS means we wouldn't have to run or maintain anything extra ourselves, since we're already fully on AWS for everything else.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to schedule a haircut, it's overdue at this point.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Returned the package, the refund should show up in a few days.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Estimated our current event volume and it's well within what SQS handles comfortably.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to call the insurance company about a claim this week.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Still a little worried about outgrowing SQS's model down the line if we ever need Kafka-style replay.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Got a reminder that my credit card statement is due soon.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My gym class got moved to a different time slot this week.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Talked to another team that outgrew SQS eventually, but they said it took them almost two years to get there.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Going with SQS for the event pipeline. Being one less thing to operate on top of our current AWS setup outweighed Kafka's extra capabilities we don't need yet.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I'll start wiring up the first queue next sprint.

query: What message queue did the team decide to use for the event pipeline?
